summary: Full e2e testing of Gimu Digital Hub - Landing page, auth, products, cart, and checkout flows all tested. 13/13 backend tests passing. Frontend renders correctly with all key sections visible.

minor.issue: Response returns user fields at root level (not wrapped in {user: ...}). Minor inconsistency - not a bug, just note for API consumers.
Email is lowercased before storing - expected behavior but undocumented.

ui_bugs.issue: Missing data-testid='product-card' attribute on product cards in ProductsPage

issues: Footer renders correctly with brand, product links, and contact info
After login, navbar shows cart icon + user icon instead of text 'Keranjang'/'Keluar' - appears to be by design (icon-based nav)
Add to cart button exists (text='Tambah ke Keranjang') but missing data-testid='add-to-cart-btn'

action_items: Add data-testid='product-card' to ProductCard component for testability
Add data-testid='add-to-cart-btn' to add-to-cart button in product detail page
critical_code_review_comments: CORS_ORIGINS is set to '*' in .env but CORSMiddleware uses allow_credentials=True - this combination is invalid and browsers will reject credentialed requests. Must set explicit origins (e.g., CORS_ORIGINS=https://medicalproduct-hub.preview.emergentagent.com). HIGH PRIORITY.
seed_admin() does not update admin if password was NOT changed (only updates if verify_password fails) - this is correct behavior.
server.py is 559 lines - borderline for splitting but acceptable.
Access token expiry is 15 minutes - refresh token flow should be tested to ensure seamless UX (token refresh on expiry).

success_rate.frontend: 95% - all pages load, all flows work, minor testid attributes missing

seed_data_creation: 10 products and 1 admin user seeded by server startup. Test user created by register test (cleaned up by unique UUID emails).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend APIs are working. Frontend is working. Key issue: CORS with allow_credentials=True and allow_origins='*' is invalid - browser will reject credentialed requests in production. Currently working in test env likely due to same-origin behavior. Fix: set explicit CORS origins.
rca_of_cors_issue: FastAPI CORSMiddleware with allow_credentials=True and allow_origins=['*'] violates CORS spec. Browsers reject this combination. Fix: change CORS_ORIGINS env var to explicit origins like 'https://medicalproduct-hub.preview.emergentagent.com' and update the middleware accordingly.
